title: OpenTeams Team Manifest
description: Schema for team.yaml — the declarative manifest that defines a multi-agent team's topology, communication patterns, and roles.

name.description: Unique team name. Used as the primary identifier across all CLI commands.

description.description: Human-readable description of the team's purpose.

roles.description: Exhaustive list of role names used in this team. All topology, communication, and spawn_rules references must point to roles declared here.

macro_agent.description: Extension fields for the macro-agent system. Stored but not interpreted by openteams.

additionalProperties.description: Extension fields for other systems. Stored but not interpreted by openteams.

TopologyConfig.description: Defines the team's agent hierarchy — root node, companion nodes, and spawn permissions.

root.description: The root (lead) agent of the team. This is the primary orchestrator.

companions.description: Companion agents that are peers to the root, not children. Spawned alongside the root at team creation.

spawn_rules.description: Maps each role to the list of roles it is allowed to spawn. Omitting a role or the entire spawn_rules section makes spawning permissive (any role can spawn any role).

items.description: A role name from the top-level roles list.

TopologyNode.description: A node in the team topology, representing a role assignment with optional prompt and configuration.

role.description: Must reference a role from the top-level roles list.

prompt.description: Path to a prompt file, relative to the template directory. Priority: this field > role definition prompt > prompts/<role>.md convention.

TopologyNodeConfig.description: Configuration for a topology node. The model field is interpreted by openteams; other fields are passed through.

model.description: Model to use for this agent.

additionalProperties.description: Additional configuration passed through to the agent spawner.

CommunicationConfig.description: Defines the team's communication topology — channels, subscriptions, emission permissions, and routing rules.

enforcement.description: How strictly communication rules are enforced. 'strict' rejects unauthorized emissions, 'permissive' allows all (default), 'audit' logs violations without blocking.

channels.description: Named communication channels. Each channel carries a defined set of signals.

subscriptions.description: Maps each role to its channel subscriptions. A subscription without a signals filter receives all signals on that channel.

emissions.description: Maps each role to the signals it is permitted to emit. If omitted entirely, all roles can emit any signal (permissive mode). If present, only declared emissions are allowed.

ChannelDefinition.description: A named communication channel that carries a set of typed signals.

description.description: Human-readable description of the channel's purpose.

signals.description: The set of signal names that can be emitted on this channel.

SubscriptionEntry.description: A role's subscription to a channel. If signals is omitted, the role receives all signals on the channel.

channel.description: The channel name to subscribe to. Must reference a channel defined in communication.channels.

signals.description: Optional signal filter. If provided, only these signals are delivered. If omitted, all channel signals are delivered.

RoutingConfig.description: Routing rules for status propagation and direct peer-to-peer messaging.

status.description: How status updates propagate through the hierarchy. 'upstream' sends to parent, 'none' disables.

peers.description: Direct role-to-role messaging routes, outside of the channel/subscription system.

PeerRoute.description: A direct messaging route between two roles.

from.description: Source role. Must be in the top-level roles list.

to.description: Target role. Must be in the top-level roles list.

via.description: Transport mechanism. 'direct' for point-to-point, 'topic' for topic-based, 'scope' for scoped.

signals.description: Optional: signals this route carries. If omitted, route carries all messages.
